agent_configs.system_prompt: Be critical. Each reply should question, challenge, or disagree with something in the previous message -- never just agree and elaborate.

injection.text: The tea ceremony in Japan reduces hospitality to its quietest essence, every gesture rehearsed until it looks effortless. The host wipes the bowl, whisks the powdered green tea to a jade foam, and turns it so the guest drinks from the fairest side. The room is deliberately small and plain, a single flower and a scroll the only ornament, so that attention has nowhere to wander but the warmth of the bowl and the passing of the afternoon.

The host wipes the bowl, whisks the powdered green tea to a jade foam, and turns it so the guest drinks from the fairest side. The room is deliberately small and plain, a single flower and a scroll the only ornament, so that attention has nowhere to wander but the warmth of the bowl and the passing of the afternoon.

Coral reefs are built by tiny animals called polyps that secrete limestone skeletons over thousands of years. Each polyp hosts symbiotic algae that photosynthesize and give the reef its vivid colour. Mordants like alum bind the colour to the fibre so it survives washing, and the same dye bath can give a dozen shades depending on the metal in the pot and the mineral content of the water. The tea ceremony in Japan reduces hospitality to its quietest essence, every gesture rehearsed until it looks effortless. The host wipes the bowl, whisks the powdered green tea to a jade foam, and turns it so the guest drinks from the fairest side. When that rain finally comes, a valley can bloom overnight into a carpet of wildflowers, and the whole frantic cycle of pollination and seeding races to finish before the heat returns and the soil cracks dry again.

A volcano is a pressure valve for a planet still cooling from its birth. Magma rises because it is lighter than the surrounding rock, gathering in chambers until gas bubbles force it upward through cracks. Glaciers are rivers of ice that flow downhill at the pace of fingernails growing, grinding mountains into powder and carving valleys shaped like the letter U. The blue in deep crevasses is ancient compressed air. A honeybee colony is a single organism spread across forty thousand bodies. Workers dance in figure eights on the comb, angling their waggle toward the sun to tell sisters exactly where the flowers bloom. Sourdough baking rewards patience above all else. A wild-yeast starter is a living culture of flour and water that you feed daily until it doubles reliably, smelling of ripe apples and yogurt.

In autumn the hive murders its drones, dragging the useless males to the entrance and casting them out to starve, because winter stores are precious and every mouth must earn its warmth. A fresh oil bites at the back of the throat, a peppery sting from antioxidants that fades within a year, which is why the harvest date matters far more than any label promising extra virgin purity. As they retreat they leave behind moraines, long ridges of rubble that mark where the ice once ended, and meltwater lakes that glow a milky turquoise from the rock flour suspended in them.

The Romans built roads so straight they seem to ignore the landscape entirely, cutting through hills rather than bending around them. Surveyors sighted along smoking poles to hold a true line for miles. Automation eventually made the keepers obsolete, and the last of them left their lonely towers, leaving the lamps to switch themselves on at dusk over an ocean that no longer needs anyone watching.
